items.title: Random variables
items.description: E[X] and Var(X) are computed from the PMF or PDF of a random variable X; you need a valid distribution before taking expectations.

items.title: Covariance and correlation
items.description: Var(X + Y) involves Cov(X, Y); covariance is defined via expectations E[(X − μ_X)(Y − μ_Y)] = E[XY] − E[X]E[Y].

items.title: Loss functions
items.description: MSE loss minimizes expected squared error; cross-entropy minimizes expected negative log-likelihood—both are expectations over data and model outputs.

items.title: RMSE and MAE
items.description: RMSE is the square root of average squared error (linked to variance of residuals); MAE is average absolute error (linked to E[|error|]).

items.title: Common distributions
items.description: Bernoulli, Binomial, Poisson, Gaussian, and other named distributions publish closed-form E[X] and Var(X) used throughout ML.

items.title: Scaling and normalization
items.description: Standardizing features to zero mean and unit variance applies sample estimates of E[X] and Var(X) before training.
